notes: Copy to ~/.config/claude-model-router/config.json and edit. Requests whose JSON body carries a `model` matching a route (exact id, or a trailing-* prefix; first match wins) are forwarded to that route's upstream; everything else — including all claude-* ids — goes to defaultUpstream with the caller's auth headers untouched. Upstreams for foreign models must speak the Anthropic API surface themselves (a translating proxy such as CLIProxyAPI). The optional `guard` block is a hard stop for metered upstreams: completion POSTs beyond `maxConcurrent` in-flight or `dailyBudget` per local day answer a non-retryable 403 telling the agent to stop and hand off to a human (reset a tripped budget by deleting guard-state.json next to this file; scope `routed` guards only route upstreams, `all` includes defaultUpstream). The optional `catalog` block shapes the merged GET /v1/models catalog only — discovery/display, never routing: `hide` (exact id or trailing-* prefix) drops matching ids (use it when an upstream advertises the same model under alias ids, e.g. bare `luna` alongside `gpt-5.6-luna`, which otherwise shows up as duplicate /model picker entries — a hidden id still forwards if a client asks for it), and `aliases` maps a model id to the display_name pickers show. NEVER put credentials in this file; the only router credential is the optional ROUTER_AUTH_TOKEN env var, required for (and only enforced with) non-loopback binds.
